title: Neillia nakatsu-riparia
intro: Neillia nakatsu-riparia, also known as Japanese Neillia, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the Rosaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and inflammation, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are beneficial for immune system function and may help alleviate respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Neillia nakatsu-riparia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, and is also available in capsule or tincture form as a supplement.
taxonomy: Neillia nakatsu-riparia is classified within the family Rosaceae. The genus Neillia is part of the order Rosales. Neillia nakatsu-riparia is a species of flowering plant native to Japan. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae and is often found in riparian zones. Neillia nakatsu-riparia is also known to be a rare species within its native range.
